summary_lines: Newcastle tender re-released.

Waiting decision, expected ????...
Following on the engagement at ECR and UKIO, the Thames Valley team have indicated that they will be coming out to tender reasonably soon.

Oxford University NHS FT,

Lots of scenarios, poor strategic alignment (lots of on-prem, separate solutions, etc).
Held meeting with SWASH 15/07/2025
SWASH looking to:
National pathology solution to be deployed phased across 40+ Labs in Southern Ireland

NIMIS (National Radiology system, delivered by Change/Optum) is currently not expected to go to tender soon, despite questions about validity and legality of the extensions ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ theyΟΔβ²§β³Δve been there for 15 years and the suggestion is that they should have gone to tender.
PIN released ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ expected to have heavy on-prem focus
Sectra secured opportunity, beating AGFA and Philips.
Covers 7 NHS Trusts (4 Sectra ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Whittington, North Midds, Moorfields and GOSH; 3 Non-Sectra ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ UCLH, Royal Free and RNOH).
Radiology + Breast Screening only for now.
Additional Ophthalmology VNA opportunity discussions ongoing with Kevan Turnbull (EA for Moorfields)
Additional options pricing provided, waiting for further updates from NCL.
Contract expected now by end of the year.
last_update_lines: Contract still being discussed ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ final schedules being agreed, but several escalations being raised by NCL on material terms still. Still aiming for 13th March signature although seeming more and more unlikely.

Consequences of delay are significant and might result in Contract not being awarded. However, I think risk is minimal and they have no alternative therefore we should press on the need for an alternative mechanism more heavily. Drafting a contract in the alloted time is simply not feasible or safe.

summary_lines: LNW and C&W migrating from Sectra on-premise to Sectra One Cloud.
Region includes Hillingdon and Imperial (CD: Anastasia Gontsarova) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ aim will be to consolidate across the region eventually as these contracts mature.
Going back to drawing board with Contracts as single contract preferred.
Deployment commenced on the back of signed Letters of Guarantee (covering full cost of MS Reservation Period).
CCN issued for warranty extension to align with new contract Go-Live.
last_update_lines: Contract fully signed! Will remove this from next week on.

summary_lines: Large private hospital group in Ireland (Dublin, Cork and Galway).

north east and north cumbria digital pat.display_name: North East and North Cumbria Digital Pathology (NENC, formally NECA)
summary_lines: The region, formerly known as NECA, has gone out to tender for a new Digital Pathology solution.
Currently a Sectra DPAT site comprising the following Trusts:
CountyΟΔβ²§¬·DurhamΟΔβ²§¬·andΟΔβ²§¬·DarlingtonΟΔβ²§¬·NHSΟΔβ²§¬·FoundationΟΔβ²§¬·Trust
GatesheadΟΔβ²§¬·HealthΟΔβ²§¬·NHSΟΔβ²§¬·FoundationΟΔβ²§¬·Trust
NewcastleΟΔβ²§¬·UponΟΔβ²§¬·TyneΟΔβ²§¬·HospitalsΟΔβ²§¬·NHSΟΔβ²§¬·FoundationΟΔβ²§¬·Trust
NorthΟΔβ²§¬·TeesΟΔβ²§¬·andΟΔβ²§¬·HartlepoolΟΔβ²§¬·NHSΟΔβ²§¬·FoundationΟΔβ²§¬·Trust
SouthΟΔβ²§¬·TeesΟΔβ²§¬·HospitalsΟΔβ²§¬·NHSΟΔβ²§¬·FoundationΟΔβ²§¬·Trust
NorthumbriaΟΔβ²§¬·HealthcareΟΔβ²§¬·NHSΟΔβ²§¬·FoundationΟΔβ²§¬·Trust
NorthΟΔβ²§¬·CumbriaΟΔβ²§¬·IntegratedΟΔβ²§¬·CareΟΔβ²§¬·NHSΟΔβ²§¬·FoundationΟΔβ²§¬·Trust
The consortium is currently on customer-provided hardware and was mandated to retender despite being broadly satisfied with SectraΟΔβ²§β³Δs existing service.
This was Sectra UKΟΔβ²§β³Δs first live DPAT site (October 2019) and was very competitively priced at the time.
A significant price increase is expected, but we have proactively aligned expectations with the customer and shared indicative business pricing earlier this year.
last_update_lines: The Trust are adamant that a contract must be signed by 31st March. We have pushed back heavily but it sounds like they are still unconvinced.
Escalation call w/ senior stakeholders to be booked next week, but there may be a need for a letter of escalation to the Trust as they are not taking no for an answer.

summary_lines: NPIC Contract coming to an end in September 2027.
They have requested various extension and reprofile options to accommodate for the increased licencing profile they are expecting.
Option include extending for 2.5yrs to align with the Expo-e contract and fully utilised the procured storage.
They have also requested a Sectra One Cloud option to evaluate moving to SaaS in 2028.
last_update_lines: CCN signed ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ will remove next week.

west midlands imaging network (wmin) ΟΔβ.display_name: West Midlands Imaging Network (WMIN) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Converged Digital Imaging Platform (CDIP)
summary_lines: The WMIN, led by University Hospitals Birmingham, has issued an RFI for a regional Converged Digital Imaging Platform.
Aims to unify PACS/RIS across 15 Trusts, supporting shared reporting, AI, and data analytics.
Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust
Sandwell and West Birmingham Hospitals NHS Trust
The Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust
The Dudley Group NHS Foundation Trust
University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust
The Royal Orthopaedic Hospital NHS Foundation Trust
Birmingham WomenΟΔβ²§β³Δs and ChildrenΟΔβ²§β³Δs NHS Foundation Trust
University Hospitals Coventry & Warwickshire NHS Trust
The George Eliot Hospital NHS Trust
South Warwickshire University NHS Trust
Worcestershire Acute Hospitals NHS Trust
Wye Valley NHS Trust
The Shrewsbury & Telford Hospital NHS Trust
The Robert Jones & Agnes Hunt Orthopaedic Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
University Hospitals of North Midlands NHS Trust
Estimated value ~Ο²¬Θ76 m (5+5 or 10-year contract).
Tender expected 2026 following current RFI and market testing.
Currently multi-vendor landscape; move toward single platform.
Sectra well positioned given regional footprint and proven success in similar multi-Trust imaging programmes (e.g. GM, NIPACS+).
Competition expected from incumbent PACS suppliers; early engagement critical.
last_update_lines: Response submitted ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ will remove this entry from next week while we wait for tender.
